data.title: ZAWYA: GlobeMed Iraq & Alyamama Insurance sign TPA agreement to enhance access to care for insured members
data.description:
GlobeMed Iraq, the leading healthcare benefits management company in Iraq, and Alyamama Insurance Company, a prominent insurer in the country, have signed a Third-Party Administrator (TPA) agreement effective 15 January, 2026, reinforcing their shared commitment to advancing health insurance services across Iraq. The signing ceremony was attended by senior executives from both organizations.

Under this agreement, GlobeMed Iraq will act as the TPA for Alyamama Insurance, providing its insured members with streamlined access to an extensive network of healthcare providers, along with a comprehensive suite of digital services and solutions.

Dr. Tarek Salibi, Assistant Vice President at GlobeMed Iraq, commented: “We are proud to partner with Alyamama Insurance and are pleased to welcome them to our growing portfolio. This agreement reflects our shared vision and ongoing efforts to deliver efficient, accessible, and high-quality healthcare services to insured members. At GlobeMed, we are driven by a strong commitment to reliability, transparency, and service excellence, and we look forward to supporting Alyamama Insurance in enhancing the overall healthcare experience of their members.”

Ms. Hanan Ismael Salman, the CEO of Alyamama Insurance Company, stated: “We are delighted to collaborate with GlobeMed Iraq. Their expertise and comprehensive TPA services will significantly enhance the value of the services we provide to our insured members. This agreement aligns with our objective of ensuring easy access to care and delivering a seamless healthcare journey to our members”.

Through this partnership, insured members will benefit from GlobeMed Iraq’s wide healthcare provider network, advanced GlobeMed FIT mobile app, and dedicated support services, including a 24/7 contact center to ensure continuous assistance and improved patient journeys.

This collaboration reflects both companies’ forward-looking approach and their commitment to setting new standards in the health insurance sector, ultimately contributing to improved access to care and higher customer satisfaction across Iraq.

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Swedish headline inflation 1.6% yr/yr in March
data.description:
Consumer prices in Sweden, measured with a fixed interest rate (CPIF), fell 0.6% in March from the previous month and were up 1.6% from the same month a year earlier, final figures from the statistics office showed on Tuesday.

The data was in line with flash figures published last week.

The statistics office said that fuel prices rose sharply in March but this was offset by a large drop in electricity prices. Food prices also fell, driven by lower dairy prices.

data.title: Norway's Nordic Halibut Q1 sales volume rises 10%
data.description:
Overview

  • Norway halibut farmer's Q1 sales volume rose 10% yr/yr, while average price per kg fell

  • Q1 revenue reached NOK 47.7 mln on 251 tonnes sold

  • Company says Tingvoll facility construction on track to support scale-up strategy

Outlook

  • Company reiterates production target of 10,350 tonnes HOG by 2031

  • Tingvoll facility expected to be fully operational by 2027 to support scale-up strategy

Result Drivers

  • HIGHER SALES VOLUME - Q1 sales volume rose 10% yr/yr to 251 tonnes

  • LOWER AVERAGE PRICE - Average sales price fell to NOK 188/kg from NOK 193/kg in Q1 2025

  • INCREASED HARVEST WEIGHT - Average harvest weight rose to 6.6 kg from 5.6 kg in Q1 2025

data.title: Green steel startup Stegra secures $1.7 billion in fresh funds
data.description:
Swedish green steel startup Stegra said on Tuesday it has raised 1.4 billion euros ($1.65 billion) in new funding from an investor consortium led by Sweden's influential Wallenberg family.

Stegra, which in 2024 said it had secured 6.5 billion euros in loans and equity, in October last year added that it was seeking an additional funds to complete its flagship hydrogen-based steel plant in northern Sweden.

The company, formerly known as H2 Green Steel, had also said it was in advanced talks about outsourcing some operations.

The Wallenberg family is Sweden's most influential business dynasty, controlling firms worth hundreds of billions dollars, including SEB , Ericsson , Atlas Copco , Electrolux and Investor AB .

The sprawling family holdings are managed via an intricate mix of private entities and holding companies, including the Knut and Alice Wallenberg Foundation and two other foundations that ultimately own FAM, and unlisted investment firm.

($1 = 0.8500 euros)

data.title: UK's AB Dynamics H1 revenue falls 16% on China weakness
data.description:
Overview

  • UK testing equipment maker's H1 revenue fell 16% due to order delays and weak China volumes

  • Adjusted diluted EPS for H1 dropped 15% on lower operating profit

  • Company launched strategic review of China VadoTech unit, recorded £16.8 mln exceptional items

Outlook

  • Company expects FY 2026 mean adjusted operating profit in line with market expectations of £24.4m

  • Company sees 55-60% of FY 2026 revenue weighted to the second half

  • AB Dynamics maintains medium-term ambition of 10% average organic growth per year

Result Drivers

  • ORDER TIMING AND CHINA WEAKNESS - Revenue declined due to delays in order intake and lower volumes at VadoTech Testing Services in China, as customer ramp-up did not occur as expected

  • COST ACTIONS AND OPERATIONAL IMPROVEMENTS - Operating margin was maintained as cost mitigation actions, operational improvements, and a positive revenue mix offset the negative impact of lower revenue

  • SEGMENT VARIATION - Testing Services revenue fell sharply due to VadoTech underperformance, while Simulation revenue was stable with higher software sales offsetting lower motion platform sales

data.title: CBOT soybeans to retest support at $11.56-3/4
data.description:
Key points:
  • For more technical analyses, please clickTECH/C

The CBOT soybean May contract (SK26) would retest a support at $11.56-3/4 per bushel, a break below which may open the way toward $11.50-1/2.

The deep fall on Monday suggests a completion of the bounce from the March 16 low of $11.45-1/4, which adopted an irregular flat pattern.

The bounce might be driven by a wave B, the second wave of a three-wave cycle from $12.38-3/4. The current wave C is unfolding toward $11.40-1/4.

Resistance is at $11.67-1/4, a break above which could lead to a gain to $11.73-1/2. On the daily chart, a bearish engulfing pattern formed between Monday and April 10, signalling a further drop.

* Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. Views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered as being business, financial, or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial, or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.

​

data.title: Imperial Brands maintains 2026 targets despite Middle East risk
data.description:
Imperial Brands maintained its annual targets on Tuesday, driven by higher tobacco pricing and growth in its smoking-alternative products, but said the potential impact from the Middle East conflict during the second half of the year remains uncertain.

The maker of Winston, Davidoff, and Gauloises cigarettes expects low-single-digit growth in its tobacco business and double-digit net revenue growth in next-generation products at constant currencies, alongside annual profit growth of 3%-5% in 2026.

data.title: SOS: Revenue up 14% to £42.3m, profit before tax £0.4m, and net cash £8.4m after buybacks
data.description:
Revenue grew 14% year-on-year to £42.3m, with own site up 24% and profit before tax reaching £0.4m. Gross margin improved, net cash rose to £8.4m after share buybacks, and strong trading continued across all channels.

Original document: Sosandar Plc [SOS] Earnings Release — Apr. 14 2026

data.title: ACG: Operational excellence and strong cash flow in 2025 set the stage for copper production in 2026
data.description:
Strong operational execution in 2025 drove robust margins and cash generation, with Gediktepe mine exceeding production guidance and advancing its copper transition. Despite a net loss from non-cash fair value adjustments, liquidity and capital structure remain strong, positioning for copper production in 2026.

Original document: ACG Metals Limited Class A [ACG] Annual Financial Statement — Apr. 14 2026

data.title: ABDP: H1 2026 saw lower revenue and profit, but strong cash, margin gains, and robust order coverage
data.description:
Revenue and profit declined in H1 2026 due to delayed orders and weak China volumes, but gross margin improved and cash generation remained strong. A £16.8m exceptional charge for Vado Tech led to a statutory loss, yet the order book and outlook support confidence in meeting full-year expectations.
